load_type.title: Load Type
load_type.description: Full load re-reads everything each run. Incremental load filters by a date field from a chosen starting point.

date_field.title: Date Field
date_field.description: Date field to filter on. Only some endpoints offer date filters.

date_from.title: Date From
date_from.description: Lower bound for the selected date field. Accepts 'last_run' (continue from last run), a relative value like 'yesterday' or '5 days ago', or an ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD).

columns.title: Columns to extract
columns.description: Optional; restrict the output table to these columns; leave empty to extract all columns; primary-key columns are always included.

probe_limit.title: Probe sample size
probe_limit.description:
Fast, read-only introspection of the UOL system for an AI agent (e.g. KAI) configuring this extractor. Returns real data quickly so you can decide how to set up extraction **before** committing to a long-running `run`.

**Two modes, chosen by whether `endpoint` is set:**
- **Catalog mode** ‚Äî invoke with no `endpoint` (e.g. from the root/connection config). Returns every available UOL object with its `primary_key`, `date_fields` and known `columns`. No data is fetched. Start here to discover what UOL exposes and which objects support incremental loads.
- **Sample mode** ‚Äî invoke with `endpoint` set (the default from a row, where an object is already chosen). Fetches up to `probe_limit` real records (default 5, max 20), flattened exactly as `run` writes them, plus the object's `primary_key`, `date_fields` and discovered `columns`.

**Use this to** confirm credentials reach UOL, enumerate objects, inspect real column names and sample values, and decide `load_type` / `date_field` / `columns` for a row ‚Äî without running a full extraction.

**Recommended sequence (agents):**
1. Catalog ‚Äî call with no `endpoint` to list objects, primary keys and available date fields.
2. Sample ‚Äî call with `endpoint` set (optionally a small `probe_limit`) to inspect real rows for the object you intend to extract.

**Mapping a probe result back into a row config:**
| Row field | Source from the probe response |
| --- | --- |
| `endpoint` | the object you sampled ‚Äî its `name` from a catalog-mode entry, or the top-level `endpoint` field returned in sample mode |
| `load_type` | `incremental_load` if the object has `date_fields`, otherwise `full_load` |
| `date_field` | one of `date_fields` (incremental only) |
| `date_from` | `last_run`, a relative value (`yesterday`, `5 days ago`), or an ISO date `YYYY-MM-DD` |
| `columns` | a subset of `columns` (the primary key is always kept) |

**Rules for agents (no server-side enforcement ‚Äî your responsibility):**
- Keep `probe_limit` small. Sync-action responses travel through the platform's stdout channel; large payloads are truncated or rejected. Default 5; hard cap 20.
- Start with catalog mode ‚Äî it makes no API call and tells you which objects support incremental loads.
- The action is read-only (GET only), but treat UOL as production data anyway.

**Result shape ‚Äî read this, agents:** the action returns the platform sync-action envelope `{"status": "success", "type": "info", "message": "..."}`. The structured payload lives **inside `message`** as a fenced ```json code block ‚Äî extract the JSON between the ```json fences and parse it.
**Payload ‚Äî catalog mode (no `endpoint`):**
```json
{"endpoints": [{"name": "sales_invoices", "primary_key": ["gid"], "date_fields": ["issue_date_from"], "columns": ["..."]}]}
```
**Payload ‚Äî sample mode (`endpoint` set):**
```json
{"endpoint": "sales_invoices", "primary_key": ["gid"], "date_fields": ["issue_date_from"], "columns": ["gid", "buyer_name"], "sample_count": 5, "sample": [{"gid": 123, "buyer_name": "..."}]}
```

**Errors:** an unknown `endpoint` exits non-zero with the list of valid objects; missing or invalid credentials surface as an authentication error. Catalog mode never calls the API and cannot fail on connectivity.

**Related actions:** `listEndpoints` / `listColumns` / `testConnection` (UI dropdowns + connection check) and `run` (the actual extraction ‚Äî asynchronous, writes output tables; not for agent-time configuration).
